title: Find it
subtitle: The Map Before the Path Exists
description: A proto-symbolic artifact documenting the formation of thought itself. It serves as a cognitive map and containment field for scattered insights, utilizing spatial thinking to discover internal structures before they stabilize into language.

motifMap.meaning: Awareness of being observed or observing; meta-cognition; the watcher of watching.
Decision-making and branching logic; movement without a clear endpoint.
Language attempting to catch up to structural insight rather than leading it.

excerpt: I found something—but it is still finding me back.

associatedMedia.description: Hand-drawn cognitive map featuring an eye and segmented paths.
associatedMedia.altText: A hand-drawn map with dense text and geometric patterns.

integrity.notes: Generated based on Phase Analysis 3.0. Identified as a threshold artifact in the Child of the Call phase. Filename: find-it.phase-the-child-of-the-call.metadata.v2.6.1.json
